decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by utilizing hatebears like Anointed Peacekeeper, Archon of Emeria, Chancellor of the Annex, and White Plume Adventurer. These creatures put pressure on the opponent while also hindering their ability to cast spells efficiently due to cards like Clarion Conqueror and Void Mirror. The deck also includes mana acceleration in the form of Ancient Tomb, Chrome Mox, and the Moxen, allowing for fast deployment of its disruptive creatures. Overall, the deck aims to be aggressive while disrupting the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on using artifacts and lands to disrupt opponents while quickly deploying threats to pressure them. It aims to lock down opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void, Null Rod, and Sphere of Resistance, while also utilizing Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland to disrupt mana bases. The deck can quickly ramp out powerful threats like Golos, Tireless Pilgrim and Lodestone Golem, and utilizes cards like Mystic Forge and Nettlecyst to generate card advantage. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents with fast mana and disruptive threats to control the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing counterspells and removal spells to control the game, while also leveraging powerful artifacts like Bolas's Citadel and Karn, the Great Creator to gain advantage. It aims to disrupt opponent's strategies while creating incremental advantage through card draw and manipulation. The deck also has ways to generate additional mana or draw cards with cards like Moxen and Lórien Revealed. By controlling the pace of the game and having multiple win conditions like Monastery Mentor and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents in the long game.

The deck utilizes the powerful Oath of Druids card to control the game and cheat out powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers using Show and Tell. It also has a strong control package with Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect its game plan. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while establishing its own threats quickly, using cards like Lotus Petal and Mana Crypt for fast mana acceleration. Overall, the key strategy is to control the game and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures.

The key concept of this deck is to dominate the game through powerful control elements, using counterspells, removal, and card advantage to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a strong board presence. By utilizing Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion, the deck gains additional value and consistency in recurring low-cost permanents. The inclusion of key Vintage staples like Ancestral Recall, Force of Will, and Time Walk further enhances the deck's ability to control the game and outmaneuver opponents. With a focus on efficiency and disruption, this Dimir Control deck aims to outplay and outlast its foes in the Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and aggressive creatures to quickly overpower the opponent. By combining powerful artifact creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler with mana acceleration from cards like Grim Monolith and Mishra's Workshop, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence early in the game. Key cards like Tezzeret, Cruel Captain and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional utility and card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Manifold Key and The One Ring enhance the deck's overall synergy and power.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping out powerful artifacts and utilizing them to create a aggressive and disruptive game plan. By using cards like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to accelerate mana production, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with threats such as Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. Manifold Key and Scrawling Crawler provide additional utility and synergy with artifact creatures, while cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck combines mana acceleration, disruptive elements, and powerful threats to create a fast-paced aggressive strategy.

The deck aims to quickly disrupt the opponent's resources while efficiently deploying threats of its own. It utilizes powerful artifacts and mana acceleration from Mishra's Workshop to cast impactful threats like Lodestone Golem and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim. Crucible of Worlds and Strip Mine provide land destruction, while Null Rod and Sphere of Resistance hamper opponent's spells. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures, disruption, and efficient mana production to secure victory in a Vintage 1 vs 1 setting.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with dredge cards like Golgari Grave-Troll and Stinkweed Imp to set up powerful graveyard recursion with cards like Narcomoeba and Prized Amalgam. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Force of Negation to protect the combo pieces. Bazaar of Baghdad helps to filter through the deck and fuel the graveyard. The goal is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and recursive threats, creating a difficult board state for the opponent to deal with.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful blue and black cards to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Ancestral Recall, the deck aims to maintain board control and card advantage. Lurrus of the Dream-Den serves as a valuable companion, providing additional utility and recursion for key cards. With a mix of disruption, card draw, and powerful threats like Tundra and Urza's Saga, this Esper Control deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through strategic play.

The key idea of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana with cards like Ancient Tomb and Mishra's Workshop, and then use that mana to play powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge. The deck also focuses on controlling the game with disruptive artifacts such as Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle. With the support of cards like Manifold Key and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain, the deck aims to create a fast and aggressive board presence to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on using artifacts and mana acceleration to quickly bring powerful creatures and disruptive spells onto the battlefield. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Scrawling Crawler while also using artifacts like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also has access to powerful artifacts like Mana Crypt and Sol Ring to generate additional mana. Additionally, cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain provide additional utility and card advantage to outpace the opponent.

The deck aims to utilize the MUD archetype to overwhelm opponents with aggressive strategies and disruption through artifacts. By combining powerful mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Ancient Tomb with key artifacts like Chalice of the Void, Trinisphere, and Sphere of Resistance, the deck can quickly establish board control and limit the opponent's options. With efficient threats like Lodestone Golem and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim, along with card advantage from Mystic Forge and Crucible of Worlds, the deck can maintain pressure and outvalue opponents in the early to mid-game.

The deck focuses on playing disruptive creatures that hate on the opponent's strategies while also being aggressive in nature. By utilizing hatebears like Anointed Peacekeeper, Archon of Emeria, and Chancellor of the Annex, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while also attacking with efficient creatures like Clarion Conqueror and Seasoned Dungeoneer. The inclusion of cards like Void Mirror and Chalice of the Void further disrupt the opponent's plays, making it difficult for them to effectively cast spells. The deck also has mana acceleration in the form of Chrome Mox, Ancient Tomb, and the Mox cycle to power out its threats quickly.

The deck focuses on utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures into play, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also includes control elements such as Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its game plan. Show and Tell can also be used as a way to cheat creatures into play. The deck aims to control the game until it can establish its combo and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures. The inclusion of cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Veil of Summer helps protect its key spells from counter magic.

This deck focuses on using Oath of Druids to quickly get powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield. It also includes a variety of control elements like Force of Will, Pyroblast, and Null Rod to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck has some card draw with Ancestral Recall and Gitaxian Probe, as well as versatile planeswalkers like Oko, Thief of Crowns and Wrenn and Six. Additionally, it has ramp with cards like Black Lotus and mana fixing with fetch lands like Misty Rainforest and Scalding Tarn.
